title: Change documentation site to use monospaced fonts
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

Please use a mono-spaced font on the documentation site, because the current font makes it difficult to distinguish between a `-` and `--` in the command line arguments.

comments.body:
If I do copy paste of some text in

http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/markdown/podman-run.1.html

with Google Chrome and Fedora 32 it looks like this

```
â€“interactive, -i=true|false
```
I think it should have been

```
--interactive, -i=true|false
```
@eriksjolund and @burhan I'd not noticed that, thanks for the heads up.  Now to figure out how to change the font there....

A few options:

* change the markdown  so that instead of bold `**--build-arg**=*arg=value*` for the arguments, it is marked up as a code block, the default Sphinx theme being used (`alabaster`) will use `'Consolas', 'Menlo', 'DejaVu Sans Mono', 'Bitstream Vera Sans Mono', monospace`, which should render better.

* override the default body font, by adding `font_family` to the `conf.py` setting for the theme options.
@burhan TYVM for the pointers, I'll go dig around for those.
@TomSweeneyRedHat, did you find a way to alter the fonts?
@vrothberg no I've not, I was sidetracked by other things.  Will try to boost this up the queue again.

@burhan do you have some MD tips you might offer? The issue I'm having is if I convert this line: ``` **--interactive**, **-i**=**true**|**false** ``` which now shows as: **--interactive**, **-i**=**true**|**false** To a code block for each of the bold fields in that line: ``` **`--interactive`**, **`-i`**=**`true`**|**`false`** ``` I see: **`--interactive`**, **`-i`**=**`true`**|**`false`** That makes the bold text too small. The only thing I could come up with is this: ``` 
--interactive, -i=true|false
``` which shows: 
--interactive, -i=true|false
Which is also too small. I tried specifying the size of the `
` font, but that was not very successful.  If it was straight html I could do something with css or `
` tags, but I'm not seeing how to make that work in md.  Thoughts?
I am going to close this issue, since we never got any additional feedback.
Just noting https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/9856 from @Luap99 which fixed this issue.
